vsV7.note: v7 baseline (2026-04-15): Lovable 79.7%, Bolt 70.9%, Cursor 83.2%, Manual 87.8%, Avg ~79.9%
vsV7.interpretation: Score impact of SLOP+SPEC additions is minimal — new rules are advisory, score penalty is modest

cat-26-rule-1.label: Placeholder comments
cat-26-rule-1.note: Below expected 60-80% — threshold may be too strict for Lovable repos

cat-26-rule-2.label: AI tool fingerprints
cat-26-rule-2.note: ZERO — public Bolt repos on GitHub typically cleaned up before publish

cat-26-rule-5.label: Mixed comment language
cat-26-rule-5.note: Expected — benchmark repos are EN-only, DE/EN mixing is a different use-case

calibrationSprint13: cat-26-rule-1: Loosen AI-placeholder patterns (too specific — misses Lovable boilerplate)
cat-26-rule-2: Add pass condition when .bolt directory absent from root
cat-26-rule-5: Skip check entirely if <5% of comments are non-ASCII (EN-only repos)

specAnalysis.assessment: FAR exceeded expectation — virtually all public repos lack AI context files and PRDs. Rule is universally relevant.

cat-18-rule-9.label: AI context file
cat-18-rule-9.note: Strong signal — validates "Context Is the New Code" thesis

cat-18-rule-10.label: PRD present
cat-18-rule-10.note: Near-universal — almost no repo has a PRD

cat-18-rule-11.label: README drift
cat-18-rule-11.note: Low FP rate confirmed — threshold of 2 packages working correctly

cat-18-rule-12.label: .cursorrules has stack
cat-18-rule-12.note: Expected — only fires when .cursorrules substantive; rule-9 fires first for most repos

calibrationSprint13: cat-18-rule-9: Weight ×2→×3 justified (hit-rate 92%, FP rate near 0%)
cat-18-rule-10: Expand skip condition — repos <20 files already skip; monitor for false context on landing pages

cat-19-rule-3.label: Git governance (severity info)
cat-19-rule-3.verdict: Expected firing — severity info correctly reduces score impact

bolt.pattern: High variation 64-79% — lower code quality ceiling than Lovable

cursor.pattern: Most consistent quality — no repos below 82%

manual.pattern: Best absolute scores — slight drop from SPEC rules (no PRD in open-source projects)

criteria.SPEC relevance: PASS — 100% of repos have ≥1 SPEC finding (PRD + AI context file universally missing)
criteria.SLOP relevance: PARTIAL — 41% hit rate (below 60-80% target); calibrate patterns in Sprint 13
criteria.FP rate: PASS — FP-fixes confirmed, no known FP issues in API/Performance/i18n rules
criteria.Score stability: PASS — +0.3pp delta; new rules do not destabilize existing scores
criteria.Code quality: PASS — 49/49 repos scanned without errors; checker stack is production-quality

nextSteps: Sprint 12: Monitor Beta users for SLOP rule FP feedback
Sprint 13: Loosen cat-26-rule-1 patterns after FP data from 10 beta repos
Sprint 13: Increase cat-18-rule-9 weight ×2→×3 (high hit rate justified)
Sprint 13: R3 threshold evaluation (currently ≥2 — may increase to ≥3)
